title: hebe cookiana
intro: Hebe cookiana, also known as the mountain hebe, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It belongs to the family Plantaginaceae and is a member of the Hebe genus. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and flowers of the Hebe cookiana plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to promote digestive health, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system.
